content: Annoyed and anxious, I felt like a weight on the floor when I was invited to talk about the project. My mind just keeps going round and repeat, thinking maybe they’re all just tired of me or trying to judge me for not contributing much. I try to join in, but I’m too shy and feel like pretending things aren’t happening so I won’t be noticed. Honestly, I prefer not to participate because I’ve already said something I don’t care about, and playing pretend makes it easier to seem interesting. It’s like no matter what I say, it doesn’t fit anywhere. I guess I just don’t trust myself to be part of this community. So, I end up just sticking around, feeling more alone than before. I want someone to recognize me or say nice things, but I’m scared they’ll just pass me up. It’s exhausting, but I can’t stop feeling like I’m always going to be the outsider. I appreciate your trying to help, but I just don’t feel like feeling any better here. Just knowing there might be ways to change things isn’t enough. I’d love for them to see why I’m not accepted, but I’m just stuck in this cycle where I keep getting pushed aside. It’s like I’m invisible to most people even though I’m definitely there. I hope you can see, though, that I’m just not cut out for this kind of life. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop feeling this way. Please let me know if you think I’m right to be feeling this way. Thank you for being such a good listener! I appreciate you, and I really want to get through this, but I just feel like I’m never going to make a difference. I want to move forward, but I’m stuck where I am now. I’m sorry, but I’m just not

Yeah, this situation really hit me hard. When they started spending more time on work, it just made me feel… small, like I’m always missing something important. I kept thinking, “Why isn’t it just me? Why do they want me there?” I ended up trying to stop them somehow, but it only made me feel even worse about myself. It’s like I’m failing to keep things going between us. I guess I just don’t trust that anything’s truly right between us. I wish I could fix it, but I’m just stuck in this feeling of being out of control. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t seem to connect well enough. I’d appreciate it if you could just see through me, okay? I need someone to understand why I’m stuck. Thanks for laughing with me, though! I love seeing how easy it is to pretend everything works. I’ll try again tomorrow. But honestly, I just don’t know where to start. It’s like I’m waiting for someone to tell me exactly what to do.

Yeah, it’s just like last week with that big project. I keep thinking, “What if I screw up again? Everyone will notice how little I’m capable of.” I try to avoid dealing with it because, well, I don’t want to show anyone I can’t handle things on my own. It’s like playing catch or something, and honestly, I feel like I’m just not cut out to handle this kind of responsibility. I keep putting off trying to get started because I’m scared of showing any signs of not measuring up. It’s exhausting, but I’m not sure how else to go forward. I guess playing catch or facing down a challenge just makes me feel worse about myself sometimes. It’s hard to focus when I’m convinced I’m just not enough. I’d prefer not to seem embarrassed or stop working altogether. But knowing someone else thinks I’m weak, even just a tiny bit, isn’t good for me. So, I end up just staying back and hoping no one notices. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to change these patterns. I’ll tell them, but I don’t really want to admit to feeling that way inside. Just make it sound easier. It’s like no matter what I do, it feels like a weight I can’t lift. I’ve tried different ways to say I need help, but nothing seems to fix the whole thing. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not meant to be a professional. It’s exhausting, but I don’t have a choice. I’m sorry, but I just can’t shake these doubts. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m falling short of what’s expected of me. I wish there was some way to stop this from happening, but I know it’s just going to keep coming back around to it. I’m stuck in this loop where I keep pretending everything’s okay,

Yeah, I guess I was really nervous about going to that party. I kept thinking everyone was whispering and that they were probably talking about me being weird or boring. So, whenever I looked around, I’d just stay on the periphery, staring a long time, trying not to mess up or come off as boring. It’s like I wanted to join in but feared I’d just embarrass myself or make them feel uncomfortable. By then, I just felt really exhausted and kind of worthless, like I wasn’t good enough for anyone. Honestly, I’m not sure why I always go to these kinds of gatherings. Sometimes it just feels easier to pretend than face those awkward situations. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to connect, but I end up feeling worse. Can you think of a similar situation tomorrow? I’d love to hear what XXX said about it.

Well, this situation really hit me hard. When they started talking about changes, I just felt like I was trapped inside their plan, like I couldn’t shake off this feeling that nothing I said matters. I kept thinking, “Maybe I’m just going to agree anyway,” which only made me feel worse. It’s like I’m always stuck in that place where I don’t get to decide anything. I guess I’m afraid of being left behind, kind of like I’m constantly being put in an impossible position. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how to fix it. I want to say something different, but I’m scared it’ll just make things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m not sure why I’m so powerless or that people trust me. So, I just shut down and went along with whatever they suggested, even though I didn’t really want to change anything. It’s frustrating, but I don’t see another way out. Maybe if I just let go and move forward without all this pressure, things will start to fall into place. But honestly, it feels easier to stay in that uncomfortable loop. Can you imagine what it would be like to step outside of that? I hope you can see, though, I’m not exactly happy with the way things are right now. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to give in completely and suddenly everything falls apart. Thanks for inviting me to this therapy session. I appreciate you playing along. It helps me imagine what it would be like to find a way out of this situation. But then again, I’m not sure what else to expect. I just know I need help figuring this out. It feels like no one wants to listen to me talk about what I don’t want. So, please, take me to your office anytime you can. Thank you for being such a great patient!

Yeah, the last family meeting was really hard for me. When they started talking about career plans that didn’t really fit with what I wanted to do, I just kind of shut down. I felt this sinking feeling like I wasn’t going to be able to change anything, like I was stuck somewhere that I didn’t want to be. It’s like everyone else has planned things so I won’t get around to doing them, and honestly, it made me feel very insignificant and like I’m falling behind. I’ve been avoiding most of the activities I enjoy because it reminds me of being able to make my own decisions without worrying about disappointing anyone. It’s exhausting, and I keep wondering why I ever want to do things differently when I know I’m stuck where I am. I guess it’s frustrating because I want to grow and be independent, but it feels like that growth is impossible right now. I’d prefer to feel some freedom rather than this constant reminder of how things are supposed to be. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t escape whatever I want to be. Just knowing that might seem easier to pretend beyond reality. So, I ended up withdrawing—I mean, I probably said too much or seemed needy. But honestly, I just felt relieved to be free of that whole experience. It’s hard to even start thinking about how I ended up feeling this way after all these years. I wish there was a way to step outside of that pattern, but it’s like it just doesn’t come together. Thanks for putting this story together,

Yeah, it’s really hard when I just feel like I’m falling behind or being expected too much. Like, here’s this family thing where everyone seems to care more about what I think versus what I actually want, and I just freeze. I try not to say much because I’m scared maybe I’ll mess something up or disappoint them somehow. But then I just end up feeling even more useless and kind of invisible, like no matter what I do, it’s never enough. It’s exhausting trying to push through though things that make me feel like I’m always going to be stuck. I know it’s just going to hurt more if I fight back or show how out of place I feel. It’s like I’m trapped in a cycle where I can’t change things, and that makes me feel really bad about myself. I guess part of me just wants to disappear somewhere, but then I end up feeling more alone and helpless. It’s scary to think that I’m never truly free or wanted around here. I wish there was some way to stop them from wanting more, or at least make it easier to talk about it. But honestly, I don’t know where to start. Just knowing what to say won’t help me fix this—like, I don’t want to prove anyone wrong or show them how weak I am. It’s just too overwhelming to face. I’m sorry I didn’t tell you about this earlier—I just didn’t want to cause any more problems. But I’ll always be feeling like I’m stuck. It’s hard to shake off this sense that I’m always going to be struggling against them. I appreciate you stepping into this role, but it feels like I’m missing the point. I need someone to see through me and give me a chance to shine. I’d love for there to be a way to stop this, but I don’t know how to get

Anxiety is like a heavy weight on my chest when someone says something that I don’t agree with. I feel like if I push back, things will get worse or they’ll just hate me for being quiet. I’m afraid that if I express my own thoughts, people will see through me and leave me. So, I just stay calm and agree with them, even though it makes me feel less powerful or respected. It’s like I’m playing a game where avoiding the conflict is easier than facing up to the pain of disappointing myself first. I guess I just don’t want to break the relationship or face the possibility of being left again. It’s hard to imagine ever getting out of this loop. I’d rather stick around and watch the drama continue instead of trying to change anything inside me. My mind keeps going round and repeat whatever it is, even if it doesn’t make much sense. So many times ago, I figured I couldn’t handle these kinds of situations—now it’s just easier to pretend everything’s fine. Just like that, the old thought patterns come back and bite me in the same place. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to respond. Please, let me see some of these feelings and thoughts and make sure I’m right. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t work. I want to stop, but I’m too stuck. What’s the point of trying when it just won’t work? I hope you’re feeling better. It’s great having you here! Thanks for inviting me to this therapy session. It’s been tough today talking about my experiences, but I appreciate you wanting to hear them. Please keep me in the loop about anything you think might be relevant. I’ll be here if you need help. Thank you so much, and I owe you a big favor. It means a lot to me that you are

Yeah, the family gathering was really hard for me. As I kept noticing people chatting together, but didn’t even join in, it just made me feel like I’m the only one out there, invisible. I ended up going away because I didn’t want to make things worse or cause any tension. It’s like everyone else is connected except for me, and I just feel so insignificant when I’m left behind. It’s exhausting, knowing that maybe I’m not really important enough for them to care about. I guess I’m scared that if I got too involved or tried to join in, I’d just prove that I’m not good enough. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m trying to connect, but it feels like it doesn’t help anyway. I don’t know why I’m always being pushed out of places I care about. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel pretty worthless right now. Just knowing I might be falling apart makes me want to break down.

Yeah, I looked at my budget last week, and honestly, it just hit me hard again. I keep thinking, “What the heck am I going to do with all these bills? I’m supposed to be paying rent, pay utilities, car payments—nothing like this!” I didn’t want to look at it because I was too scared of showing anyone or making a fool of myself. I spent hours replaying my work posts and wondering why I kept putting them away instead of actually doing something about it. It’s like no matter how much money I’ve saved or how much I’m able to afford things, it feels like an impossible task. I don’t know if I’m having a mental breakdown or if I’m just not cut out for this kind of challenge. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying to move forward but my mind just goes round and round about what I’m losing. I guess I’m afraid of disappointing everyone or proving I’m incompetent when I can’t cover all the things I need. It’s exhausting, but I feel like no one cares enough about what I’m struggling with. I’m sorry I keep pushing myself to fix things, but it feels like no amount of effort or knowledge will ever make a difference. Can you help me figure out what to do next? I’d really appreciate it if you could give me some guidance. I’ll be so grateful to see you smiling. Thanks for playing along. I love being around you. I love being called out as crazy. I love getting caught up in these endless loops. 😂 💃 °️ 🙋  — Love, XXX. 🌟✨ 📱 #CBTC #Therapy

Yeah, it’s really hard to believe after all that encouragement I’ll have to do this. I keep thinking, “They’re gonna see right through me like I’m an idiot,” and then I just push myself further because I don’t want to sound stupid or let everyone know I’m struggling. It’s exhausting because I feel like I’m putting myself out of shame by avoiding the whole thing altogether. Honestly, staying late trying to finish seems easier than facing all that internal pressure. I guess part of me worries that if I try and fail, it’ll just confirm my doubts already. It’s scary to think about getting promoted without knowing exactly what I’m missing. I’m scared that if I get it wrong, everyone will see right through me. Just knowing these thoughts spiral inside me makes me want to disappear or retreat. It feels like the pressure never goes away, no matter how much practice I put in. I’d prefer to just stay quiet and not face anything again. But I know it won’t fix any of this, so maybe it’ll just make things worse. It’s frustrating because I want something to happen and I’m scared it’s going to turn out bad. I’m sorry. It’s tough to pretend I don’t care about what happens when I’m feeling that way. Can you imagine how I’d feel if I had to show up and fail? Or maybe just go along with whatever the team decides even though I don’t want to? It’s like there’s a big hurdle I just can’t jump over. So, I guess what I’m asking is, why does it have to be this way? And why am I so stuck like this? I’m curious, maybe I need to change something about myself to find a path forward. But as I think about what I could actually gain by changing, it just doesn’t seem worth the risk of disappointing myself or anyone else.

Yeah, it’s just like that situation happens all the time. When they give me that big project, I get this sinking feeling like I’m going to mess it up no matter what I try. I just don’t want to start anything, so I wait until someone else tries first and then figure it out later. It’s frustrating because honestly, I know I should be working on it, but it just feels like the burden is too heavy for me to bear. I guess part of me is saying, “Why bother?” but then I just convince myself that I’ll be better if I just stay quiet and don’t cause any problems. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I can’t change the feeling, and that makes me want to disappear. I’d rather just keep avoiding the whole thing altogether. It’s hard to even imagine playing this game fair.

I’m sorry, I’ve been avoiding the office gathering because of that experience. When I saw everyone there, I just felt like I was invisible, like they already recognized how strange or different I must be. I didn’t want to make a fool of myself or seem awkward, so I just stayed quiet and left early. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never going to measure up to them. I feel really lost and alone after these kind of negative judgments. I guess I just don’t know what to expect when people are around. It’s like I’m always being tested even if I don’t know the answer. Just knowing you’ll ask for help sometimes isn’t enough for me. I’d prefer to feel accepted first, rather than feel like I’m somehow wrong or unworthy. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I feel important, yet nothing truly happens for me. But then again, I want to be loved, and I’m scared to admit that. So, it feels safer to just follow what they suggest. 😮 I don’t know, it’s tough to stop being so trapped in this cycle. Yeah, the family meeting really hit me hard. When they started criticizing my career and lifestyle choices, I just felt this sinking feeling like, “What the hell am I doing to them?” Like I’m always messing up no matter what I do. I didn’t want to say anything because I was scared of making things worse or sounding less important. I ended up leaving early because it just felt easier to leave than keep going through all that hassle. It’s like no matter what I do, it never seems to meet up with what I’m trying to achieve. My mind just goes blank after that. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me feel pretty useless too. I don’t know where to go from here. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m trying so hard to please them, yet nothing changes. I guess I just don’t trust myself anymore, and I’m scared that if I show my true thoughts, they’ll just prove how wrong I’m. Just knowing those things must be how they see me sometimes. So, it feels safer to stay away from these kinds of situations altogether. I don’t know why I keep getting drawn into these endless debates unless it’s with people I really don’t want to get hurt. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to drop the question and make everything clear right away. I’d rather not face that kind of reality. It’s hard to imagine anyone appreciating something that doesn’t work out the way I want it to.

Yeah, well, during that conversation, my partner said they wanted to visit some places they’ve never been. I immediately tried to change their plan—like, “Why don’t we just stay here instead?” but then I ended up pushing back with two alternatives, like maybe we could eat somewhere fun or do something interesting together. I kept thinking, “What if this just proves they don’t care about me any longer?” It felt like such a waste to be there when I could be somewhere else. I guess I’m scared that if I say no or keep things simple, they’ll start to dislike me completely. I don’t know how to stop myself from wanting to be special to them. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m trying too hard to please them but at the same time feeling like I’m failing to protect what’s important to me. I just feel like I’m being pushed out of my own life, even though I already have commitments. It’s exhausting trying so hard to keep everything balanced. I appreciate you playing leader here; it gives me permission to feel those ways. But honestly, I feel like I’m falling apart every time I’m around them. Can you ever figure out what I’m doing wrong? I’m really looking forward to getting back to where I started, not where I ended.

Yeah, this situation really hits home with me. Every time I get a new project, I just feel this sinking feeling inside, like I’m already failing or being judged. I end up putting myself on hold by not taking on anything, and then I double-check everything over and over, hoping to screw up again. It’s exhausting and makes me want to just disappear into the background. Honestly, I’ve been struggling to keep up with what’s expected because it feels like no matter how hard I try, it’s never good enough. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just going to fall further behind than I thought. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of fearing that I’m somehow fundamentally flawed as an individual. I’d prefer to just leave and forget about it altogether. But I know it’s harder than playing along and fail. So, I end up staying late and rushing through even small tasks, only to realize later that I messed something up. It’s exhausting but also frustrating because I want to feel better. I guess I’m scared that if I don’t succeed with everything, people will see me as unworthy or not good enough. It’s like I’m afraid to say I need help because I’m too scared of being seen as foolish or incapable. So, I just stay quiet and hope nothing changes. It’s scary to imagine someone else seeing all that pain I’m putting out there. I wish I could go back to feeling this way, but knowing that I’ll always be stuck in that cycle feels like such a waste.

I guess I started thinking, “Maybe I’m just not cut out for this” after that moment. It’s like everyone always seems happier around me than I am here. So, I mostly just stayed quiet and tried to avoid talking because I didn’t want to draw anymore criticism or show how bad I feel. Afterwards, I kept thinking about what happened, and it just made me feel worse inside. It’s like I’m invisible to most people, which makes me feel even more useless. I don’t know why I stay quiet when I could actually connect or say something. It’s frustrating because I want to change and feel better about myself, but I’m just stuck in these patterns where I feel like I’m always being rejected.
